title: Affordable Yacht Charter in Marbella: 2026 Costs Guide
html:
An affordable yacht charter in Marbella is easier to find than most visitors expect: sharing a half-day skippered motorboat from Puerto Banus marina can land in the EUR 90 to EUR 160 per-person band, and BoatHire24 lists the verified boats that make those days possible along the Costa del Sol.

Where to set off in Marbella

Marbella sits at the centre of a coastline thick with launch points, so your choice of departure marina shapes the whole day.

  • Puerto Banus: the glamorous flagship marina, busy with motor yachts and steps from the bars.
  • Puerto Deportivo de Marbella: the town's central marina, quieter and close to the old quarter.
  • Puerto Cabopino: a smaller harbour east of town beside the Artola dunes.
  • La Bajadilla: the working port that also hosts leisure and fishing trips.
  • Estepona marina: roughly 25 nautical miles west, a relaxed base for a longer run.

From any of these you can reach La Concha's backdrop, the sandy bays toward Cabopino, or the open Alboran Sea.

Which boat to rent in Marbella?

The cheapest comfortable option is a shared catamaran day or a small skippered motorboat, while groups tend to scale up to a mid-size cruiser. Match the hull to the day you actually want.

  1. Shared catamaran tour: best for couples wanting a sociable, stable day; many guests; from EUR 45 per person.
  2. Small motorboat (no-licence): best for short self-drive hops; up to 6 guests; from EUR 180 per half-day.
  3. RIB or speedboat with skipper: best for dolphin-spotting at pace; up to 8 guests; from EUR 350 per half-day.
  4. Mid-size motor yacht: best for groups wanting shade and a cabin; up to 11 guests; from EUR 900 per day, and it is worth taking time to compare the Princess V58 in this class.
  5. Sailing yacht: best for a calmer wind-driven day; up to 8 guests; from EUR 600 per day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella?

Affordable days cluster around shared tours and small motorboats, from roughly EUR 45 per person. Prices swing with boat size, season and whether a skipper and fuel are included, and you can find a skippered charter once you have set your dates and group size.

  • Shared group tour: roughly EUR 45 to EUR 70 per person for a high-season half-day.
  • Small motorboat, half-day: from about EUR 180 plus fuel for up to 6 guests.
  • Catamaran, full day: typically EUR 1,200 to EUR 2,500 depending on size and crew.
  • Crewed motor yacht: commonly EUR 2,000 to EUR 6,000 per day for larger vessels.

How does booking a Marbella charter actually work?

You choose your dates and party size, message the operator to confirm details, then pay securely online before the boat is held for you. Verified listings show clear inclusions, so there are no surprises.

If you are new to chartering, read how deposit, skipper and fuel policies differ between boats, then search available boats to filter by date, capacity and price before you commit.

What is the best season to charter in Marbella?

The high season runs from June to September, when warm settled weather and calm mornings make for the easiest days afloat. Spring and early autumn are cheaper and often just as pleasant.

On the Costa del Sol, mornings are usually calmest. The afternoon Levante, an easterly wind funnelling in from the Strait of Gibraltar, can build a short chop, so a morning slot in July or August often means a smoother trip.

Spanish waters generally require the skipper to hold a valid licence for larger or faster boats, while many small motorboats can be hired without one; the operator confirms the exact requirements for each vessel before you book.

At a glance

  • Affordable price band: from EUR 45 per person shared, EUR 180 half-day motorboat
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours half-day, 8 hours full day
  • Guest capacity: 6 to 12 on common rental boats
  • Currency: EUR
  • Main departure point: Puerto Banus, Marbella

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I rent a boat in Marbella without a licence?

Yes, several small motorboats under a set engine power can be hired without a licence, typically for self-drive trips close to shore; the operator confirms which boats qualify and what coastal limits apply on the day.

How many people fit on an affordable charter?

Most budget-friendly motorboats and RIBs in Marbella carry between 6 and 12 guests, while shared catamaran tours take larger numbers; capacity is listed on each boat, so check it matches your group before booking.

Are dolphins really seen off Marbella?

Dolphins are seen regularly in the Alboran Sea off the Costa del Sol, especially on calmer mornings; sightings are never guaranteed, but skippered trips often head toward known feeding grounds.

Is fuel included in the price?

Fuel is sometimes included and sometimes added on top, depending on the boat; smaller self-drive rentals usually charge it separately, so confirm the policy in writing when you book.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own. Weigh up the season against the shared tours and the larger yachts, then set your dates and check availability across local boats before they fill for the summer.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
